description:
Cleanup ticket — file now, do after 22.12 lands.

fn_constants (HashMap<Var, FnId> per spec, src/ir_typer.rs) was introduced as a parallel mechanism to track \"this Var is exactly fn F\" because the type system couldn't express it. closure_lit (22.8) is exactly that expression. The two mechanisms now overlap.

## Scope

1. Audit every reader of fn_constants:
   - rewrite_known_target_closures (src/ir_typer.rs:860+)
   - phase-1 walker's CallClosure-with-known-target branch (line ~725)
   - cont_slot0_descr's CallClosure branch (line ~779)
   - codegen chain_repr's resolve_sid_under path
   - any others surfaced by grep

2. For each, replace fn_constants[v] lookup with closure_lit extraction from env.get(v). Verify equivalence on every fixture.

3. If equivalence holds across the test suite, delete fn_constants from FnTypes, delete callsite_fn_consts propagation, simplify rewrite_known_target_closures or delete it (if codegen-direct in 22.11 fully covers the rewrite intent).

4. If equivalence does NOT hold for some reader (rare edge case where fn_constants tracks something singletons don't), document the gap and keep fn_constants as a narrow shim — but never as a parallel general mechanism.

## Acceptance

- cargo test --workspace green.
- src/ir_typer.rs no longer carries an unmotivated parallel map. Either fn_constants is gone, or its remaining scope is documented in code.
- Lines of code net negative.

## BLOCKED BY

- fz-ul4.27.22.12 [[fz-cps-seam.force-drop]]

comments.text:
Victory-lap audit (post-22.12):

fn_constants has 21 reader sites across ir_typer.rs (plus 3 unit tests
asserting on it directly). closure_lit subsumes the *closure-target
resolution* role but fn_constants also:

- Tracks resolved FnIds for direct fn-name references via the
  unification in rewrite_known_target_closures (which still operates
  on the IR pre-codegen and isn't trivially driven from per-spec
  Descrs).
- Propagates through cont captures via callsite_fn_consts
  (per_param[k+1] = caller_ft.fn_constants[captured[k]]); without
  this the cont's body sees an opaque-typed closure-var.
- Is asserted on directly by unit tests that pre-date closure_lit.

Closing as deferred. Replacing the readers one by one would require
either:
  (a) Threading the spec map through every callsite that today only
      has access to a single FnTypes (rewrite_known_target_closures
      currently iterates all specs to unify — a per-spec rewrite is
      a different design).
  (b) Removing rewrite_known_target_closures entirely and depending
      solely on codegen-time per-spec dispatch (which 22.11 already
      does for TailCallClosure; CallClosure non-tail is still indirect
      so the rewrite still has work to do there).

Both are coherent refactors but each is its own ticket-sized scope,
not a victory-lap simplification. The duplication is documented:
closure_lit is the canonical singleton type; fn_constants is a
narrower per-Var shortcut that survives because rewrite_known_target_closures
hasn't been ported.

Re-file the focused work as fz-ul4.27.22-followup when there's
appetite to rebuild rewrite_known_target_closures on Descr.

title: fz-cps-seam.retire-fn-constants: retire parallel fn_constants once closure_lit subsumes it
